description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a steep blue gable roof and beige horizontal siding. The structure features a prominent front porch with wooden railings and steps leading up to a main entry door, which is sheltered by the roof overhang. Windows are framed in white with brown trim and are arranged asymmetrically on each facade, providing natural light to both floors. The house includes a small upper balcony accessible from the second floor, visible above the porch. Downspouts and gutters are attached to the roof edges, channeling rainwater away from the foundation. The overall design is simple and functional, with a focus on practicality and modest decorative elements.
description: A beige two-story house with a steep blue gable roof, front porch, and asymmetrical white-trimmed windows. Features include a wooden balcony, entry steps, and practical rainwater drainage.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the steep blue gable roof, beige siding, and three white-trimmed windows arranged asymmetrically.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the long blue roof, a downspout, two windows, and the side of the covered porch.
description_view_2: The front view displays the main entry with steps, a covered porch, balcony above, and several windows with brown trim.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the blue roof, beige siding, two windows, and a downspout, with no porch visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry with steps, covered porch, wooden railings, and balcony above
